rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BISHOP COHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Specially Appearing Movants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Prooof Of Service Of Notice Of Petition For Final Distribution, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Judge Joseph M. Quinn. On January 10, 2022, Evered Cohen as Administrator of the Estate of Bishop Cohn, Sr., filed his Second and Final Report and Account of Administrator and Petition for (1) Settlement and Approval; (2) Approval of All Acts of the Administrator; (3) Award of Balance of Statutory Compensation to Administrator and His Attorneys and Reimbursement of Costs Advanced; (4) Award of Extraordinary Compensation to Administrator's Attorneys; and (5) Final Distribution ("Initial Petition"). The clerk set the Initial Petition for hearing on April 6, 2022. Prior to the hearing date, the Probate Examiner issued notes identifying defects and deficiencies with the Initial Petition; the defects included Administrator Cohen and counsel's failure to file a proof of service of the Initial Petition. Administrator Cohen and counsel failed to respond in any way to the Examiner Notes. In order to give Administrator Cohen and counsel more time to respond to the notes, the court continued the hearing on the Initial Petition to May 4. Again, Administrator Cohen and counsel failed to respond and the court, again, continued the hearing, this time to June 15, adding to calendar status of close of estate. On June 8, 2022, the Administrator filed Administrator's Status Report and Petition for Order Continuing the Administration of the Estate for Six Months, with hearing set for October 25, 2022. On June 14, the court issued an order to show cause directing Administrator Cohen and counsel to address the defects and deficiencies with the Initial Petition identified in the Examiner's Notes. The court also directed Administrator Cohen and counsel to show cause why sanctions should not be imposed on them for failing to respond to the Examiner's Notes and why their June 8 Status Petition should not be stricken. The hearing on the Initial Petition was continued to July 27 and the OSC was set for hearing on the same day. On July 20, 2022, the Administrator filed his Amended Second and Final Report and Account of Administrator and Petition for Order: (1) Settling and Approving Account; (2) Approving All Acts of Administration; (3) Awarding Balance of Statutory Compensation to Administrator and His Attorneys and Reimbursement of Costs Advanced; (4) Awarding Extraordinary Compensation to Administrator's Attorneys Related to Appeal; (5) Surcharging Distributive Share of Heir Frank Lance Cohen for Costs of Pursuing Unsuccessful Appeal, and (6) for Final Distribution (Amended Petition"). Administrator Cohen and counsel purported to notice the Amended Petition for hearing on July 27; but, of course, such notice was insufficient given that notice of a petition must be given fifteen days before the hearing (see Prob. C., section 1220, subd. (a)(1)). Administrator Cohen and counsel filed a proof of service stati9ng that he served the Amended Petition on Angie Broughton, Frank Cohen, and Danzel Cohen by mail. On July 27, 2022, the court continued the hearing on the Amended Petition to October 11, 2022, to allow Administrator Cohen and counsel to provide appropriate notice. The court continued the OSC to the same day. [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BISHOP COHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Specially Appearing Movants' Motion To Quash Prooof Of Service Of Notice Of Petition For Final Distribution, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] In the meantime, on May 4, 2022, Specially Appearing Respondents Angie Broughton, Frank Cohen, and Danzel Cohen ("Moving Parties") filed the instant motion to quash serve of the Initial Petition, relying on Probate code section 1220 and Code of Civil Procedure section 418.10, subdivision (a)(1). Moving Parties argue they are entitled to notice of the hearing, presumably in the manner required for service of summons, on the Initial Petition as intestate heirs known to be directly interested in the estate. Given Administrator Cohen and counsel's failure to serve the Initial Petition in the manner required for service of summons, the Moving Parties argue, the court lacks jurisdiction over them. Administrator Cohen and counsel failed to file an opposition. The motion is DENIED. Section 418.10, subdivision (a)(1) of the Code of Civil Procedure applies only where a party is required to serve a summons or serve papers in the manner required for service of summons. Administrator Cohen was not required to serve a summons, nor was he required to serve the Initial Petition on Moving Parties in the manner required for service of summons. (See Prob. C., section 1215 [service by mail is sufficient].) Section 1220 of the Probate Code sets forth requirement for notice of hearings; it does not authorize motions to quash or set forth a rule regarding personal jurisdiction. Administrator Cohen was, however, required to serve the Initial Petition on Moving Parties. But, here, the Initial Petition has been superseded by the Amended Petition, thus Administrator Cohen's failure to demonstrate that he served the Initial Petition, while troubling, is of no immediate legal effect and Cohen's failure does not strip the court of jurisdiction over this estate. The motion, therefore, is denied. Administrator Cohen and counsel are ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 2, 2022. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although the Court is aware that Lorraine Iveli has requested that the status hearing be continued. The Court cannot continue a hearing based on the request of one party without all interested parties stipulating to a continuance. Examiners will not respond to any correspondence from one party in a contested matter. No Final I&A has been filed yet. No report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration has been filed yet. [Probate Code Section 12200.] The Partial No. 1 I&A filed on 6/6/22 was not properly executed by either the executor or his attorney: he did not date his verification; and she did not date the Statement About the Bond. (Also, item 4 of that I&A was completed in error when a Probate Referee was appointed.) Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAKO U ROVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/12/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copy of the amended petition file stamped on 7/18/22 but not actually e-filed until 7/21/22 (when it was available for viewing and after which notice should have been mailed) or the proof of service also filed on 7/18/22 has been received yet by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Notice was insufficient, as 7/21/22 was not at least 15 days before this hearing. No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 41.59 E.] No proof of publication of the amended petition has been filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed confidentially and conventionally. A friend does not have priority before kin (or even the Public Administrator) if a decedent died intestate. [Probate Code Section 8461.] Email addresses of interested parties are not proper addresses. [Probate Code Sections 8110 and 1215.] In the initial petition, it was disclosed that some heirs resided in Japan. If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Section 8113.] (There might be additional issues with the amended petition; it was only cursorily reviewed online to try to set forth all issues to be addressed without multiple continuances. No further papers will be reviewed online going forward.) The Court is also aware that objections were filed on 7/27/22. No courtesy copy of the objections has been received by the assigned examiner either as of 7/28/22. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (and any other pleadings filed by any party) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/3/22 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT FISHER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Jeffrey Mulhall, the decedent's grand-nephew and one of the heirs in Las Vegas, does not appear to have been served with a summons, as the amended proof of service on file does not include a signed return receipt or other evidence of actual delivery required by CCP section 417.20(a) for service of process by certified mail under CCP section 415.40. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CAROL ANNE HAMBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Authorizing Petitioner To Refinance Community Property Personal Residence
rulings.Rulings: granted without hearing by the judicial officer after review.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KRISTINE B. MORGAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/14/22. (No earlier availability.) Notice was mailed on 7/22/22, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. Proper notice must be given for the 9/14/22 hearing. The will mentions a separate personal property memorandum. In a supplement to the petition, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner, he must address whether or not such a writing was found with Decedent's personal records. If any such writing exists, it must also be lodged and the beneficiaries must be disclosed (as with other beneficiaries listed in item 8 of the petition) and given notice. A courtesy copy of the proof of service and the supplement ust be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 8/31/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUTH JOHNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance required. No conformed copy of signed order will be returned (no self-addressed stamped envelope provided). Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 08/07/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 08/21/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHIU CHUNG LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 8/17/22. no later than noon on 8/8/22, Counsel must file: an amended Duties and Liabilities form that includes petitioner's telephone number; the confidential attachment to the duties and liabilies form; and a supplement, duly signed and verified by the petitioner that identifies the relationship to the decedent of ever person listed in #8 of the petition, explains why this estate is only being opened now, almost 10 years after the decedent died, and what has been happening in and to the real property during those 10 years.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ADRIENNE MARIE MORELLO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/12/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain why San Francisco is the proper venue. It appears Decedent died as a resident of Daly City in San Mateo County. 2) Please explain why a petition for probate has been opened for approximately $25,000.00 when Probate Code 13100 allows for administration of estates of this size without a probate. 3)Please file proof of publication. 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 5) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 6) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/29/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE S BROSIUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 3/25/15. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 8/15/23. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 8/15/23, no appearance will be required on 8/15/23.
